company.name: CervoMed
company.subtitle: Neflamapimod DLB Phase 3 H2 2026 (pending financing) · Stroke + ALS expansion · $36M micro-cap
company.cashRunway: Into Q3 2026 only — Phase 3 requires financing

assets.name: Neflamapimod — p38α inhibitor (DLB)
assets.stage: Phase 3 ready (FDA aligned, H2 2026 start pending financing)

market.notes: DLB is 2nd most common progressive dementia after Alzheimer's (~1.4M US patients). No approved disease-modifying treatments. Phase 2b RewinD-LB: missed primary (bioavailability issue Batch A), BUT extension with Batch B showed 52% lower CDR-SB worsening at 16wk, 64% risk reduction in low-AD subgroup (p<0.001), 82% in pTau181<21. GFAP -16.0 (p<0.0001). FDA aligned: ~300 pts, 32wk randomized + 48wk OLE. Dose: 50mg TID stable crystal form.

us.patientsK.note: Lewy Body Dementia Association/NINDS: ~1.4M US dementia with Lewy bodies; 2nd most common neurodegenerative dementia.

eu.patientsK.note: EU DLB registries: ~1.6M DLB EU27+UK; underdiagnosed vs AD.

row.patientsK.note: Global: ~7M DLB; no DLB-specific approved therapy; cholinesterase inhibitors used off-label.

us.wtpPct.note: ~55% - Medicare Part D covers donepezil/rivastigmine generics off-label; antipsychotic sensitivity limits options.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~40% - EU rivastigmine approved for PDD, used DLB off-label.

row.wtpPct.note: ~12% - Japan covered; LMICs minimal DLB-specific management.

us.priceK.note: Blended $2.5K: donepezil/rivastigmine generics $1K (80% of pts), pimavanserin $30K for DLB psychosis (~10%).

penPct.note: ~20% peak - off-label AD therapeutics; alpha-synuclein pipeline (Lundbeck, Roche) expanding addressable.

us.reachPct.note: RewinD-LB Ph2b miss Dec 2024; Ph3 pending redesign; DLB ~400K US prevalent, addressable ~150K mild-mod -- early launch ~20% reach if Ph3 succeeds 2028+.

us.priceK.note: ~$35K/yr projected; anchored to Leqembi $26.5K + cholinesterase generics; DLB orphan premium possible if exclusivity.

us.wtpPct.note: ~35% -- no DLB-specific drug approved; Nuplazid (Acadia) covers DLB-psychosis subset; Medicare Part D for oral neurology asset, PA expected.

eu.reachPct.note: ~18% EU5; EMA path post-Ph3; DLB SOC donepezil generic, high unmet need per ParkinsonNet/LEAD guidelines.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~18% -- no DLB asset approved EU; NICE/G-BA access via specialist memory clinics only.

row.reachPct.note: <5% ROW; Japan PMDA DLB interest growing (McKeith criteria adoption high).

row.wtpPct.note: ~8% -- Japan NHI covers novel neuro, China DLB awareness low.

market.notes: ~800K ischemic strokes/yr in US. No approved therapies for stroke recovery (only acute tPA). Neflamapimod targets synaptic dysfunction underlying recovery failure. RESTORE Phase 2a enrolling, data H2 2026. Massive unmet need.

us.patientsK.note: AHA 2024 Stats: ~800K US stroke/yr (~610K first-ever); ischemic ~87%, hemorrhagic ~13%.

eu.patientsK.note: EU stroke registries: ~1.1M stroke/yr EU27+UK; thrombectomy centers expanding.

row.patientsK.note: WHO 2023: ~12M stroke/yr globally; leading cause of disability.

us.wtpPct.note: ~90% - Medicare Part A/B covers tPA, tenecteplase, thrombectomy; secondary prevention antiplatelets generic.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~80% - EU broad stroke care; tenecteplase increasingly replacing alteplase per ESO guidelines.

row.wtpPct.note: ~35% - Japan broad coverage; LMICs limited thrombectomy access.

us.priceK.note: Blended $12K acute: tPA/tenecteplase $6K, thrombectomy device $25K (20% of ischemic); 2ndary prevention generics $0.5K.

eu.priceK.note: ~$7K blended; EU device/drug net ~60% of US.

row.priceK.note: ~$2K blended; Japan near EU, rest generics.

penPct.note: ~40% peak acute treatment - thrombectomy-eligible window (24hr) and LVO selection cap device penetration.

us.reachPct.note: Acute ischemic stroke post-tPA neuroprotection; ~800K US strokes/yr, targets ~200K thrombectomy-eligible; Ph2 readouts gated 2026+, early commercial reach <10%.

us.priceK.note: ~$15K per treatment course projected; priced as acute inpatient add-on to tPA/thrombectomy bundle.

us.wtpPct.note: ~30% -- neuroprotection space graveyard (NXY-059); Medicare DRG hospital bundled; requires acute IV dosing in ER/NICU.

eu.reachPct.note: <8% EU5 early reach; EMA neuroprotection path unproven; SITS registry data required.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~15% -- EU stroke DRG bundled; NICE skeptical of neuroprotection class post-failures.

row.reachPct.note: <5% ROW; Japan edaravone comparator limits novel neuroprotection uptake.

row.wtpPct.note: ~8% -- Japan edaravone precedent, China NRDL acute stroke coverage limited.

market.notes: ALS affects ~30K US patients. Desperate unmet need — median survival 3-5 years. p38α involved in motor neuron degeneration. EXPERTS-ALS trial initiation H2 2026. Competitors: Relyvrio (withdrawn), Qalsody (antisense), Radicava.

us.patientsK.note: ALS Association/MDA: ~30K US prevalent ALS, ~5K new/year; median survival 3-5 years from dx.

eu.patientsK.note: EU ALS registries: ~40K prevalent ALS EU27+UK; ~7K incident/year.

row.patientsK.note: WHO/ALS global: ~220K prevalent ALS worldwide; Japan PMDA approved Radicava and Qalsody.

us.wtpPct.note: ~70% - Medicare Part B Radicava IV/oral, Qalsody (tofersen) for SOD1 ~2% of ALS; riluzole generic first-line.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~45% - EMA approved Qalsody 2024; Radicava withdrawn EMA; riluzole generic dominant.

row.wtpPct.note: ~20% - Japan broad coverage; emerging markets riluzole only.

us.priceK.note: Blended $40K: riluzole generic $2K (90% of pts), Radicava $170K, Qalsody $182K (SOD1 only ~2%).

eu.priceK.note: ~$20K blended; Qalsody EU ~60% of US list, riluzole generic.

row.priceK.note: ~$5K blended; Japan near EU, rest generics.

penPct.note: ~25% peak - branded neuroprotection capped by modest efficacy; SOD1 ASO and C9orf72 pipeline expanding genetic subsets.

us.reachPct.note: ALS ~30K US prevalent; neflam Ph2 exploratory; Relyvrio withdrawal 2024 leaves gap; Qalsody (tofersen) SOD1-only; reach 15% if Ph3 succeeds 2029+.

us.priceK.note: ~$120K/yr projected; Radicava ORS $171K precedent; orphan premium.

us.wtpPct.note: ~35% -- ALS orphan + ALSA advocacy; Medicare ESRD-like access; Radicava IV $145K precedent but narrow uptake.

eu.reachPct.note: ~12% EU5; Radicava withdrawn EU 2019, riluzole generic SOC; NICE high bar post-Relyvrio.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~15% -- NICE ALS historically restrictive; G-BA riluzole generic anchor.

row.wtpPct.note: ~10% -- Japan PMDA ALS precedent via Radicava.

market.notes: Rare neurodegenerative disorder — progressive loss of language. No approved treatments. Small market but high pricing potential (rare disease). Phase 2a topline H2 2026.

us.patientsK.note: NINDS/AFTD: ~30K US primary progressive aphasia; variant of frontotemporal dementia spectrum.

eu.patientsK.note: EU FTD registries: ~40K PPA EU27+UK; logopenic/nonfluent/semantic variants.

row.patientsK.note: Global: ~200K PPA; overlaps FTLD and AD pathology; no approved PPA-specific therapy.

us.wtpPct.note: ~30% - no approved PPA Rx; speech therapy and off-label AD drugs; anti-amyloid if logopenic AD-variant.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~20% - supportive care; EMA no PPA-specific approvals.

row.wtpPct.note: ~8% - minimal access; diagnosis rate low globally.

us.priceK.note: Current <$3K (off-label AD generics + speech therapy); pipeline progranulin (GRN) therapies $200K+ if approved.

penPct.note: ~15% peak - genetic FTD subsets (GRN, C9orf72) drive pipeline; small addressable, high KOL concentration.

us.reachPct.note: Primary progressive aphasia ~50K US, subset of FTD spectrum; orphan indication, Ph2 exploratory; <10% reach at launch, specialist-driven.

us.priceK.note: ~$45K/yr orphan FTD-PPA premium; anchor to Austedo/donepezil + orphan exclusivity.

us.wtpPct.note: ~30% -- no PPA-specific Rx; Medicare Part D off-label donepezil baseline; FTD advocacy (AFTD) supports orphan access.

eu.reachPct.note: <8% EU5; ultra-orphan FTD-PPA niche, ENROLL-HD/GENFI referral network.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~12% -- no PPA Rx EU; NICE ultra-orphan HST possible.

row.reachPct.note: <4% ROW; Japan PMDA FTD interest limited.

mega_bear.narrative: Cannot raise capital — Phase 3 never starts — liquidation or reverse merger
events: ✗ Capital markets shut for micro-cap biotech — no financing available
✗ Phase 3 DLB never initiates — FDA alignment wasted
✗ RESTORE stroke and ALS trials abandoned
→ Forced reverse merger or liquidation at ~$1/share (cash floor)

bear.narrative: Phase 3 funded but DLB fails — bioavailability issue was a warning, not a fix
events: ✗ Phase 3 DLB funded via dilutive raise at $3-4/share
✗ Phase 3 misses primary endpoint — Batch B efficacy doesn't replicate
✗ Stroke and ALS Phase 2a signals too weak for pivotal
→ Stock collapses post-failure, cash burn with no viable asset

base.narrative: Phase 3 funded + DLB shows meaningful slowing + stroke/ALS generate interest
events: ✓ Phase 3 DLB funded via partnership or non-dilutive grant + small raise
✓ DLB Phase 3 enrolls on time — biomarker-enriched population
~ RESTORE stroke shows encouraging signal but needs larger trial
~ ALS and nfvPPA generate preliminary data

bull.narrative: DLB Phase 3 hits — first disease-modifying DLB therapy + neuro platform
events: ✓ Strategic partnership funds Phase 3 — minimal dilution
✓ DLB Phase 3 meets primary endpoint — Breakthrough path to approval
✓ Stroke recovery Phase 2a positive — pivotal planned
✓ p38α platform validated across neurodegeneration — multi-indication

psychedelic_bull.narrative: Neflamapimod becomes the Keytruda of neurodegeneration — Biogen/Lilly acquires
events: ✓ DLB Phase 3 best-in-class: disease modification + symptomatic benefit
✓ FDA grants Accelerated Approval in DLB — first ever
✓ Stroke + ALS data transformative — p38α validated across neurodegeneration
✓ Biogen or Lilly acquires CervoMed for $3-5B (platform value)

lastData.title: RewinD-LB Phase 2b: missed primary but extension data compelling
points: Primary endpoint missed — CDR-SB at 16wk (bioavailability issue Batch A)
BUT Batch B extension: 52% lower CDR-SB worsening at 16wk
64% risk reduction in clinically significant worsening (low-AD subgroup, p<0.001)
82% lower worsening in pTau181 <21 pg/mL patients
FDA aligned on Phase 3: ~300 pts, 32wk randomized + 48wk OLE
50mg TID stable crystal form selected (fixed bioavailability issue)

nextCatalyst.title: Phase 3 DLB initiation (PENDING FINANCING) + stroke/ALS data
thresholds: Phase 3 financing: partnership, offering, or grant — existential for thesis
RESTORE stroke Phase 2a topline — first expansion readout

risks: CASH CRISIS: $27M funds only into Q3 2026 — Phase 3 requires $50-80M+
Phase 2b technically MISSED primary — all bullish data is from extension/subgroups
Bioavailability issue with original batch raises manufacturing/formulation risk
DLB trials are extremely hard to enroll (diagnosis difficulty, comorbidities)
Neurodegenerative Phase 3 trials have >80% failure rate historically
Micro-cap ($36M) — any financing will be massively dilutive at $4/share
p38α mechanism has been tried before in other neuro diseases (failures)
9.3M shares outstanding — thin float, high volatility, low institutional ownership

catalysts.title: RESTORE ischemic stroke Phase 2a topline results
catalysts._rationale: Company expects topline data from RESTORE ischemic stroke trial in second half 2026

catalysts.title: Neflamapimod nfvPPA Phase 2a topline results
catalysts._rationale: Company confirmed initial topline clinical data from nfvPPA trial expected in second half 2026
